feed.title: Unsplash
feed.subtitle: Combined Unsplash feed: blog, API changelog, and (with an UNSPLASH_ACCESS_KEY) fresh wallpaper photos.

entry.title: Top 20 Images on Unsplash from June 2026
entry.content: 20 incredible submissions from the past month

entry.title: Photo & 3D contest: Nostalgia
entry.content: A global photography & 3D render contest on Unsplash

entry.title: 9 Billion Downloads
entry.content: Not bad for a free image library.

entry.title: Top 20 Images on Unsplash from May 2026
entry.content: 20 incredible submissions from the past month.

entry.title: Illustration Contest: Single Hue
entry.content: A global illustration contest on Unsplash

entry.title: The Smithsonian is now on Unsplash
entry.content: Real art. Real artifacts. Real history.

entry.title: Unsplash Around the World: Australia 🇦🇺
entry.content: Through the lens of 5 Australia-based contributors

entry.title: Top 20 Images on Unsplash from April 2026
entry.content: 20 incredible submissions from the past month.

entry.title: Photo & 3D Contest: Optimism
entry.content: A global photography & 3D render contest on Unsplash

entry.title: Behind 2 Billion with Matthew Stephenson
entry.content: We caught up with contributor Matthew Stephenson on how it feels to have reached 2 billion views on Unsplash.

entry.title: Top 20 Images on Unsplash from March 2026
entry.content: 20 incredible submissions from the past month.

entry.title: Illustration Contest: Drawn from Nature
entry.content: A global illustration contest on Unsplash

entry.title: Top 20 Images on Unsplash from February 2026
entry.content: 20 incredible submissions from the past month.

entry.title: Unsplash Around the World: Greece 🇬🇷
entry.content: Through the lens of 4 Greece-based contributors

entry.title: Top 20 Images on Unsplash from January 2026
entry.content: 20 incredible submissions from the past month.

entry.title: Illustration access enabled for all API partners
entry.content: All API partners can now access illustrations. Previously, this required a specific permission. Endpoints that return photos will now include illustrations as well.

entry.title: Removal of order_by from GET /photos
entry.content: The photos Editorial feed will no longer support the order_by parameter, instead only returning photos in the latest order (the existing default).

entry.title: Unsplash Source sunset
entry.content: Unsplash Source has been officially unsupported since its deprecation in 2021. As part of the final sunsetting, we will first wind down by disabling the search feature, and in the coming weeks turn off the application entirely.

entry.title: Unsplash Source being deprecated
entry.content: Unsplash Source is being deprecated. Existing uses will continue to work, however for new projects use the full Unsplash API .

entry.title: Tags property from search endpoints deprecation
entry.content: The tags property from the /search and /search/photos endpoint is deprecated and will eventually be removed. This was an undocumented field and is superseded by tags_preview

entry.title: GET /collections/featured removal
entry.content: Following up on its deprecation in September, GET /collections/featured has been removed.

entry.title: Query string added to photo download_location URL
entry.content: Query string parameters have been added to the photo’s download_location URL. This may cause issues for client implementations adding their access token to the URL via string concatenation.

entry.title: BlurHash added to photo objects
entry.content: All photo objects from the Unsplash API now include a blur_hash string. This is a compact representation of an image which can be used as a placeholder before the real image loads.

entry.title: Added topic endpoints
entry.content: We have added new endpoints for topics:

entry.title: Collections ID change and featured deprecation
entry.content: Two changes to the Collections API:

entry.title: Support for languages beta
entry.content: We are adding support for non-english input languages to the search endpoint.

entry.title: Dynamic Client Registration Support Added
entry.content: For decentralized applications, like open source CMSs WordPress and Ghost, we’ve added support for dynamic client registration, which allows developers to grant individual API keys to users with a user-friendly signup process.

entry.title: User likes statistics deprecation
entry.content: The ‘likes’ data in the /users/:username/statistics response has been deprecated. The total value will be returning 0 going forward, with the entire fragment (i.e., including historical info) to be removed in the near future.

entry.title: Deprecation of ordering by ‘popular’
entry.content: We are deprecating the option to order photos by popular on all endpoints (e.g., GET /photos ). While the functionality will still exist in the immediate future, it will be soon be removed.

entry.title: Content filtering
entry.content: We’ve added a new section to the API documentation to publicly clarify our content safety guidelines :

entry.title: New filtering and ordering options
entry.content: GET /search/photos adds support for orientation, dominant color filtering, and relevancy vs latest ordering:

entry.title: Deprecated endpoint EOL: curated photos, old search, photo stats
entry.content: Three deprecated endpoints have been removed:

entry.title: Curated Collections removal
entry.content: The deprecated Curated Collection endpoints have been removed. This includes:

entry.title: API parameter resizing deprecation
entry.content: The custom cropping and resizing parameters accepted by the get a photo , get a random photo , and get a user endpoints are being deprecated and replaced instead with dynamic image URLs which are capable of more resizing and cropping options.

entry.title: Abbreviation of top photos in users search and user endpoints
entry.content: For performance reasons we are removing some of the deeply-nested photo objects returned in the search/users and users/:username endpoints.

entry.title: Categories removal
entry.content: Categories endpoints have been removed.

entry.title: Curated Collections deprecation
entry.content: Curated Collections are now deprecated. This includes endpoints:

entry.title: Abbreviation of current_user_collections
entry.content: For performance reasons we are removing some of the deeply-nested objects returned in the current_user_collections photo attribute. current_user_collections returns a list of collection objects, which will no longer include the user or cover_photo information.

entry.title: Categories EOL
entry.content: Categories endpoints won’t yet 404, but are returning empty data structures. e.g., /categories/:id/photos => []

entry.title: Instagram username now public
entry.content: Both full and abbreviated user responses now include the instagram_username attribute without requiring the read_user scope.

entry.title: Filter photo search results by orientation
entry.content: The /search and /search/photos/ endpoints will now accept an orientation parameter, and return photos only matching that. Valid values are landscape , portrait , and squarish .

entry.title: Updated API Guidelines
entry.content: We’ve updated the official Unsplash API Guidelines to include two new technical guidelines for all API applications:

entry.title: Existing deprecations
entry.content: Some endpoints and behaviour have already been deprecated, and are no longer officially supported:

entry.title: Reactor online. Initializing API changelog.
entry.content: We’ll be posting changes to the API starting from here. You can always find the current state of our API at https://unsplash.com/documentation
